title: Foreign aid given for emergencies and longer-term development
subtitle: Committed bilateral [official development assistance (ODA)](#dod:oda) from governments and multilateral organizations, grants from civil society organizations. Aid for emergencies, such as natural disasters and wars, is often called humanitarian aid. This data is expressed in US dollars and adjusted for inflation.
caption:
Foreign aid given for emergencies and longer-term development, DAC countries, 1971 to 2024

Committed bilateral official development assistance (ODA) from governments and multilateral organizations, grants from civil society organizations. Aid for emergencies, such as natural disasters and wars, is often called humanitarian aid. This data is expressed in US dollars and adjusted for inflation.

Between 1971 and 2024, the total volume of foreign aid provided by DAC countries shows a sustained upward trajectory, with the area chart distinguishing between assistance for longer-term development and aid for emergencies. The vertical axis measures committed bilateral official development assistance in constant 2023 US dollars, ranging from zero to over $200 billion. The dark green area, representing aid for longer-term development, forms the vast majority of the total throughout the period. In 1971, total aid stood at roughly $50 billion, with the green section comprising nearly all of it. By 2024, the total reached a peak of approximately $185 billion. A pink area representing aid for emergencies appears along the bottom axis, remaining negligible until the late 1990s. From 2000 onwards, this pink band widens, reaching roughly $30 billion by 2024. The green section expands significantly after 2000, rising from around $60 billion to over $150 billion in 2024. A sharp spike in total aid is visible around 1991, reaching nearly $100 billion before falling back. Another notable increase occurs after 2015, pushing the total above $140 billion. The highest point on the chart occurs in 2024, where the combined height of the green and pink areas reaches its maximum. The data shows that while emergency aid has grown in absolute terms, longer-term development aid remains the dominant component of foreign assistance.
